brief_justification: The XML is missing the article body (only metadata, abstract, and references are present). Based on the abstract, this is a qualitative study tracing the internationalization of Brazil's Public Prosecutor's Office (MPF) and its connections to the global anti-corruption field. The paper uses documentary evidence (cooperation agreements, overseas travel records) in an interpretive/sociological framework (Bourdieu, Dezalay & Garth). It makes no quantitative analysis and has no explicit method section. It implicitly frames the promotion of anti-corruption models as resulting from corporate cooperation strategies.
